name: claude-semaforo
description: Semaforo visivo per lo stato di Claude Code

commands.title: Claude Status: Set up Claude Code hooks (this machine)
Claude Status: Send a test Telegram message

enumDescriptions: Always notify, even when the VSCode window is focused
Only when the VSCode window is not in the foreground
claudeSemaforo.notifications.when.markdownDescription: When to show a desktop notification on status change.

claudeSemaforo.notifications.sound.markdownDescription: Play a sound with the notification.

claudeSemaforo.notifications.states.markdownDescription: Which status changes trigger a notification.

claudeSemaforo.telegram.enabled.markdownDescription: Also send a message to Telegram on status change (great for when you're away from the computer). Requires **botToken** and **chatId** below. Uses the same `notifications.states` filter but ignores window focus.

claudeSemaforo.telegram.botToken.markdownDescription: Telegram bot token from [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather). Stored in plain text in settings.

claudeSemaforo.telegram.chatId.markdownDescription: Your Telegram chat id (message the bot once, then run the command **Claude Status: Send a test Telegram message**, or use [@userinfobot](https://t.me/userinfobot)).

claudeSemaforo.staleWorkingTimeoutSecond.markdownDescription: Safety net for interruptions: Claude Code emits no hook when you interrupt (Esc), so the light would stay 🔴 forever. If the status stays **working** for this many seconds without any update **and no tool is running**, it's treated as finished (🟢). A running tool (e.g. a long build) still stays 🔴. Set to `0` to disable.

claudeSemaforo.sessionTimeoutMinutes.markdownDescription: Multi-session cleanup: a session whose state hasn't updated for this many minutes is treated as closed (Claude Code doesn't always emit `SessionEnd` on abrupt close) and its dot is removed. A clean exit removes it immediately. Lower = closed sessions disappear sooner; higher = idle-but-open sessions linger longer. Set to `0` to never auto-drop.
